0_Overview: What is TypeScript: Typed Superset of JavaScript
Benefits: Catch Errors at Compile Time, Not Runtime
Benefits: Superior IDE Support — Autocomplete, Refactoring, Go-to-Definition
Benefits: Safer Refactoring in Large Codebases
Benefits: Works with All JavaScript Libraries via @types
Drawbacks: Build Step Required (tsc or bundler)
Drawbacks: Learning Curve for Complex Generic Types
Drawbacks: Type Definitions Can Lag Behind Libraries
Drawbacks: Verbose in Some Cases (type gymnastics)
When to Use: Any project > 1 developer or > 1 month lifespan
When to Use: Large-scale frontend (React, Angular, Vue)
When to Use: Node.js backends where correctness matters
When NOT to Use: Quick scripts, prototypes, tiny utilities
TypeScript Ecosystem: tsc, ts-node, tsx, esbuild, SWC
